title: Minuartia picta
intro: Minuartia picta, also known as Painted Sandwort, is a flowering plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infections. The bioactive constituents of Minuartia picta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although the most traditional preparations involve the use of the plant's roots or leaves in a decoction or infusion, often used to treat digestive issues and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Minuartia picta is classified within the family Caryophyllaceae, commonly known as the pink or carnation family. This family is a group of flowering plants that includes several species of mossy stonecrop, pearlwort, and other related genera. Minuartia picta is a member of the genus Minuartia, which comprises around 40 species of flowering plants. The genus Minuartia is often divided into two main groups: the subgenus Minuartia and the subgenus Arenaria. Minuartia picta is native to mountainous regions in Europe.
